column1: Q7_other: How much does each of the following factors attract you to go fishing at a particular site?
ability to catch and KEEP fish species present.
being able to keep some fish to feed my family

being able to use barbed hooks to catch my fish and not barbless in order to loose my fish because when fighting a fish using a barbless hook and in such case loosing so called fish it will then be tired and has a greater chance of being eaten by a seal
Being outdoors, casting, understanding how to fish

Catching rainbow trout that are pink meat and native due to cut throat/rainbow mix  and i have caught many over 5 pounds and a few over 25" which cant be beat anywhere in the us including alaska. The 29" 12 # rainbow i caught and the ones that got away
getting out and doing what i love
I know people there & no public access
I like the solitude and beauty of fishing in Oregon
It was great until you closed hatchery at fall creek for your studies.
Landing fish need barbed hook so not to release ingered fish
launch ease, boat to campsite from bank accsess, toilets
Like the area and go RVing there
no other factor, i've only went once
remoteness, I wish there was more access to high lake fishing and information
salmon fish hatcheries upstream of bay
The chances of catching is the main thing for the grand ki8ds and us guys as a group.
trying to figure out regulations on the species
We live on the Columbia, but seeing SeaLions destroy more fish in one day, than we are allowed to keep in a year, is disgusting, as they are not indingenous to this area and should be removed!
Weather conditions and time of year
weather, season of year, boat launch capability
Wherever the guide says the fish are biting.
being able to keep a fish
cost ie added few, boat launch
ease of access due to handicap
engoy the day, don't care if I catch fish or not
fish food;food bill; save on food bill
hatchery fish available to keep for food.
health of the river, population of fish, solitude
how close it is to work
I fish off my deck of floating home
I Live 10 min from the Rogue River
I live on North Umpqua, it is my back yard.
more access for handicapped my wife (fishin buddy) is in a power chair
nice ramps, fish cleaning station, clean boat and motor wash station to remove invasive species
people should be allowed to fish until everyone has their limit
the ability to teach a kid in silence of nature
